faqs.q: Is SK hynix going public?
faqs.a: SK hynix is already a public company — its shares trade on the Korea Exchange in Seoul. The US move is a secondary listing: the company has filed a Form F-1, amended July 6, 2026, to list American Depositary Shares on Nasdaq. Filing existence is verified on SEC EDGAR.

faqs.q: When is the SK hynix US listing (IPO) date?
faqs.a: The page reports no listing date. The status is Filed: an F-1 registration with amendments through July 6, 2026, verified on SEC EDGAR. All reported deal terms remain subject to amendment until pricing.

faqs.q: What is SK hynix's valuation and how much would the US listing raise?
faqs.a: No standalone IPO valuation is stated. The reported deal size is net proceeds around $28 billion via roughly 177.9 million ADSs, each one-tenth of a Korean share — capped near 2.5% of the company, as Korean holding-company law requires SK Square to keep at least a 20% stake. Terms remain subject to amendment until pricing.

faqs.q: What ticker and exchange will SK hynix use in the US?
faqs.a: The reported ticker is SKHY, for American Depositary Shares on Nasdaq, while the primary listing stays in Seoul on the KRX. The ticker is a reported term, not EDGAR-verified — only filing existence, form types and dates are verified on SEC EDGAR — and remains subject to amendment until pricing.

faqs.q: What are related public AI stocks?
faqs.a: The page names three listed tickers in the same lane: MU, NVDA and SNDK. SK hynix is described as the world's leading high-bandwidth-memory maker and Nvidia's primary HBM supplier, so these public names sit along the same AI-memory supply chain. This is educational context, not a recommendation.

violations: FAQ 2 (listing date): 'No listing date has been announced' is a factual claim not present on the page. The page only shows the status badge 'Filed · F-1 (amended 2026-07-06)' and never mentions a listing date or any announcement about timing — rule 1 violation.

FAQ 4 (ticker/exchange): 'The reported ticker is SKHY ... per the amended F-1 tracked on SEC EDGAR' attributes the ticker to the EDGAR-verified F-1. The page marks SKHY only as a 'reported ticker', and its disclaimer states that only 'filing existence, form types and dates' are verified on SEC EDGAR while reported deal terms 'come from the filing via financial media'. Implying EDGAR verification of the ticker is a claim not supported by the page — rules 1/2 violation (overstated verification of a 'reported' figure).
